_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Skill to ensure that the collection strategy leverages all available resources.
reference_document_element_description: Ability to make strategic resource allocation decisions under uncertainty, balancing proven approaches with experimental initiatives and adjusting course as evidence emerges.
comments: DCWF ksa intersects with WIDAI LS domain. LS-A-003 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: DG-S-009 (DG, cosine: 0.446), DQ-S-006 (DQ, cosine: 0.433).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 3.6/10 (raw 0.364). No substantive shared vocabulary — relationship is purely semantic. DCWF-only terms [available, collection, ensure, leverages, resources, skill] vs WIDAI-only terms [ability, adjusting, allocation, approaches, balancing, course] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.872 (P=0.882, R=0.862). Balanced P/R — the 11-word DCWF element and 21-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (99.8%). "Skill to ensure that the collection strategy leverages all available resources." and "Ability to make strategic resource allocation decisions under uncertainty, balan" are related but neither implies nor contradicts the other. Standard result for cross-framework mapping where two independent frameworks describe overlapping competencies with independent vocabulary.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.485 > STS 0.364 (gap +0.121). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [none] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 3.2%. Zero substantive shared tokens. The entire relationship is semantic — no lexical trace. DCWF terms: [available, collection, ensure, leverages, resources, skill]. WIDAI terms: [ability, adjusting, allocation, approaches, balancing, course].

signals.detail: Bi-encoder/STS gap 0.121 suggests some vocabulary-driven inflation in candidate selection.
